title: crocus keltepensis
intro: Crocus keltepensis, a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of Turkey, is a type of flowering bulbous geophyte in the iris family, Iridaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Crocus keltepensis include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The herbal preparations of this plant include decoctions and infusions, which can be used to create teas and tinctures for medicinal purposes.
